trivia_bullets.text: Iona's Judgment originated in Worldwake (February 2010) as a Zendikar-block card. [Scryfall: prints search] This Commander Legends printing (November 2020) is a reprint — the Worldwake version predates Iona's lore role in the Eldrazi crisis by over a year in real time, from a period when Zendikar's angels were still largely defined by their Shield of Emeria mythology. [Scryfall]

Flavor text: 'Beneath the gaze of angels, only the righteous may stand without fear.' [Scryfall] — The phrase inverts Iona's actual lore tragedy: Iona's gaze was ultimately powerless against the Eldrazi, and even her legacy 'had become a tool for their cause.' [WebSearch: Iona MTG Fandom page snippet] The flavor text presents divine certainty; the lore records divine failure.
sources: WebSearch: Iona MTG Fandom page snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Exile target creature or enchantment for 4W sorcery. At 5 mana, this is one of the less efficient white removal spells — broad exile (creatures OR enchantments) is its appeal. Pauper-legal, penny-legal. [Scryfall] EDHREC rank 22533; Commander Legends was the first draft set designed for Commander, giving this pauper-quality card unusual exposure in the format.

related_cards.name: Iona, Shield of Emeria
related_cards.note: The named source character; a 9-mana legendary angel banned in Legacy for color-locking opponents

related_cards.name: Iona's Blessing
related_cards.note: Also in the inventory; sister Iona-named card from the same plane

related_cards.name: Banishing Light
related_cards.note: Direct mechanical comparison — efficient white exile enchantment often discussed in the same slot
